steps.title: Identify the growth model
steps.content:
The bacteria double every hour. Doubling means the count is multiplied by 2 for each hour that passes. Starting from 44,000:
After 1 hr: $$44,000 \cdot 2$$
After 2 hrs: $$44,000 \cdot 2^2$$
After $t$ hrs: $$y = 44,000 \cdot 2^t$$

steps.title: Eliminate wrong structures
steps.content: In exponential growth $y = a \cdot b^t$, the base is the growth factor (2) and the coefficient is the starting amount (44,000). Any formula that swaps these roles or uses $\frac{1}{2}$ as the base would model decay, not growth.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• A: $y=\frac{1}{2}(44,000)^t$ - base is 44,000, coefficient is $\frac{1}{2}$. Both roles are wrong. Eliminated.
  • B: $y=2(44,000)^t$ - base is 44,000, not 2. Wrong structure. Eliminated.
  • C: $y=44,000\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^t$ - coefficient is correct but base $\frac{1}{2}$ models halving (decay), not doubling. Eliminated.
  • D: $y=44,000(2)^t$ - coefficient 44,000 is the initial count, base 2 is the doubling factor. That matches Choice D — $y=44,000(2)^{t}$- D.
